content: Sepsis diagnosis requires: 1) Confirmed or suspected infection; 2) Evidence of organ dysfunction (SOFA score components: PaO2/FiO2 ratio, platelets, bilirubin, mean arterial pressure, Glasgow Coma Scale, creatinine/urine output). qSOFA screens for sepsis risk but has limited sensitivity. Biomarkers: procalcitonin aids bacterial vs viral differentiation and antibiotic stewardship decisions; lactate indicates tissue hypoperfusion. Blood cultures (2 sets, aerobic+anaerobic) before antibiotics when possible. Imaging to identify source. Differential diagnosis includes non-infectious SIRS causes (pancreatitis, trauma, burns). Sequential organ assessment guides prognosis and treatment escalation.
